status_detail: Significantly scaled down from original 120 MW plan to a 20 MW first phase. Originally announced in January 2021 as a 40 MW project, expanded to 120 MW in 2022 with >300 MW solar and wind, then scaled back to 20 MW. Target production now ~10,000 tonnes/year by 2029, subject to confirmation of European and French subsidies and public authorizations. TotalEnergies also signed separate €150M deal with Air Liquide for 25,000 t/yr to the same biorefinery.

data_management.narrative_summary: Masshylia is a green hydrogen project at TotalEnergies' La Mède biorefinery in Châteauneuf-les-Martigues, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France. Originally announced in January 2021 as a 40 MW project by TotalEnergies and ENGIE, it was expanded to 120 MW in 2022 before being significantly scaled back to a 20 MW first phase, with a new target of ~10,000 tonnes/year of green hydrogen by 2029, subject to subsidies. The hydrogen would replace fossil-based hydrogen in biofuel production (biodiesel and sustainable aviation fuel). The project was designed to pioneer digital piloting for continuous hydrogen supply with real-time solar management and large-scale storage. As of 2026, FID has not been taken and technology selection is not confirmed.

sources.title: Total and ENGIE partner to develop France's largest site for the production of green hydrogen from 100% renewable electricity
sources.author: TotalEnergies / ENGIE
sources.notes: Original project announcement with 40 MW capacity and >100 MW solar

sources.title: Engie, TotalEnergies scale down green hydrogen project — Masshylia

sources.notes: Confirms scale-down to 20 MW first phase, 2029 target, subject to subsidies

rag_metadata.text_for_embedding: Masshylia is a green hydrogen project at TotalEnergies' La Mède biorefinery in Châteauneuf-les-Martigues, Provence, France. Jointly developed by TotalEnergies and ENGIE. Originally 120 MW plan scaled back to 20 MW first phase, targeting ~10,000 t/yr green hydrogen by 2029 for biofuel production, subject to subsidies. Technology not yet selected (likely PEM). Solar-powered. Pre-FID planned project. France. Europe. refinery. Planned. 20MW.
